issues.title: Propagate Ingress annotations to generated HTTPProxy resources (parity with net-istio)
issues.body:
## Summary

`net-contour` does not propagate annotations from the parent `KIngress` to the generated `HTTPProxy` resources. This is inconsistent with `net-istio`, which does propagate annotations, and means that third-party controllers that watch `HTTPProxy` annotations (such as [external-dns](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/external-dns)) cannot be driven from `DomainMapping` or `KIngress` annotations.

## Current behavior

In `pkg/reconciler/contour/resources/httpproxy.go`, the `ObjectMeta.Annotations` of the generated `HTTPProxy` is hardcoded to only contain the Contour ingress class annotation:

```go
Annotations: map[string]string{
    ClassKey: class,
},
```

No annotations from the parent `KIngress` are propagated.

## Expected behavior

Annotations from the parent `KIngress` should be propagated to the generated `HTTPProxy`, similar to how `net-istio` handles this in `MakeIngressVirtualService`:

```go
// net-istio/pkg/reconciler/ingress/resources/virtual_service.go
vs := &v1beta1.VirtualService{
    ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
        ...
        Annotations: ing.GetAnnotations(),
    },
    ...
}
```

## Motivation / use case

A common use case is driving [external-dns](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/external-dns) from `DomainMapping` annotations. For example, to create latency-based Route53 DNS records, users add annotations like:

```yaml
apiVersion: serving.knative.dev/v1beta1
kind: DomainMapping
metadata:
  name: my-app.example.com
  namespace: my-namespace
  annotations:
    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/set-identifier: my-app-us-east-1
    external-dns.alpha.kubernetes.io/aws-region: us-east-1
```

With `net-istio`, these annotations flow through `KIngress` to the `VirtualService`, where external-dns picks them up. With `net-contour`, the generated `HTTPProxy` only has the `projectcontour.io/ingress.class` annotation, so external-dns ignores them entirely.

## Suggested implementation

In `MakeHTTPProxies`, merge the parent KIngress annotations into the generated HTTPProxy's annotations, while preserving the existing `ClassKey` annotation:

```go
annotations := make(map[string]string, len(ing.Annotations)+1)
for k, v := range ing.GetAnnotations() {
    annotations[k] = v
}
// Contour class annotation always takes precedence
annotations[ClassKey] = class

base := v1.HTTPProxy{
    ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
        ...
        Annotations: annotations,
        ...
    },
    ...
}
```

Knative-internal annotations (e.g. `serving.knative.dev/`, `autoscaling.knative.dev/`) could optionally be filtered out to avoid noise, similar to how `net-istio` filters labels.

pullRequests.title: Merge HTTPProxy routes when multiple Ingress rules target the same host
pullRequests.body:
Previously, multiple Ingress rules for the same host would create duplicate HTTPProxy objects with identical names, causing reconciliation conflicts. This bug was triggered after https://github.com/knative/serving/pull/16259 has been merged.

net-contour assumed (like net-kourier) that every hostname appears only in a single rule, which is not a correct assumption.

This change deduplicates HTTPProxy objects by name and merges routes from multiple rules into a single proxy. This enables use cases like having separate rules for main traffic and ACME challenge paths.

This PR restores the existing behavior (serving contour-latest CI should pass again) but does not attempt to fix the problem, that (in theory) different TLS/CORS/... settings could exist per [kingress+visibility+hostname combination](https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-contour/blob/84c1bccdb3bd6a6f9e28007d9f1b013158541f21/pkg/reconciler/contour/resources/httpproxy.go#L318) which is used as the key.

## Changes

- Use map to deduplicate HTTPProxy objects by name
- Merge routes when multiple rules target same (host, visibility, class)
- Sort output by name for deterministic ordering
- Add test for multiple rules with same host (ACME scenario)

/kind bug

<!-- Please include the 'why' behind your changes if no issue exists -->

## Release Note

<!--
:page_facing_up: If this change has user-visible impact, write a release note in the block
below. Include the string "action required" if additional action is required of
users switching to the new release, for example in case of a breaking change.

Write as if you are speaking to users, not other Knative contributors. If this
change has no user-visible impact, no release-note is needed.
-->
```release-note
Fix reconciliation conflicts when multiple Ingress rules target the same host
```

/cc @dprotaso

Release 0.22 version of #564

This is a fix for [CVE-2021-32783](https://github.com/projectcontour/contour/security/advisories/GHSA-5ph6-qq5x-7jwc).

Process: I read https://github.com/projectcontour/contour/compare/v1.17.0...v1.17.1 and attempted to apply the patch to the vendored copy of Contour. Unlike the real fix, I didn't provide a configmap value to override to allow ExternalName services, so an upgrade to 1.17.1 or 1.18 will be needed to re-enable the functionality.

https://github.com/evankanderson/contour/tree/CVE-32783 contains the fix; Contour is only maintaining the latest release branch.

pullRequests.title: Rename ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding to differ from existing contour installation
pullRequests.body:
# Changes

This PR renames a ClusterRole currently called `contour` and updates its references in ClusterRoleBindings. It also renames the two ClusterRoleBindings that reference the renamed ClusterRole, mainly to avoid collateral effects in case you deploy Knative (version containing the ClusterRole renaming change) over an older version. You would get an error due to changes in an immutable field (roleRef).

The motivation for this PR is to minimize changes necessary to have Knative/net-contour/contour alongside with another contour installation. Currently, if a tool like `kapp` is used to install Knative or Contour, you will get an ownership error because another app already owns a ClusterRole called `contour`.

PS.: I originally submitted these changes using the [PR#496](https://github.com/knative-sandbox/net-contour/pull/496), but I messed it up 😬  and accidentally closed the PR.

:broom: Update or clean up current behavior

/kind enhancement

Fixes #495
